element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Multiple lives available; primary traversal mechanic
Basic enemy type; establishes combat fundamentals
Secondary enemy type; variant combat challenge
Map display utility; navigation aid for later Death Star sections
Resurrection mechanic (5 uses); supports Han/Leia gameplay

element_introduction_order.element: Mos Eisley Bar / Han Solo collection
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Alternative character with single life; risk/reward mechanic
Resource management for Millennium Falcon sequence
Paradigm shift to first-person; resource-dependent survival; spatial reorientation

typical_relief_triggers: Safe transitions between major level sections
Multiple lives for Luke (early game cushion)
typical_tension_triggers: Enemy encounters in open Tatooine areas
Asteroid field navigation (resource-gated, first-person perspective shift)
Death Star interior with single-life character Han Solo or Princess Leia in play
Objective-focused Death Star sections with hostile encounters
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Exposition-driven early game (Tatooine collection sequence) transitioning to escalating mechanical and narrative intensity; paradigm shift (2D-to-3D first-person) creates mid-game tension peak; Death Star climax maintained through character-life resource scarcity

difficulty_curve.early_game: Gentle introduction via Tatooine hub; multiple Luke lives allow experimentation; basic enemy types (Stormtroopers, Tusken Raiders) establish combat baseline without severe punishment
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Paradigm shift to first-person asteroid field acts as difficulty spike; resource management (shield collection) becomes critical; spatial disorientation from perspective change increases challenge
difficulty_curve.late_game: Death Star sequence escalates via character permadeath mechanics; Han Solo and Princess Leia each have single lives only; Obi-Wan resurrection pool (5 uses) is exhaustible resource; objective-driven encounters demand sustained performance
notable_spikes: Millennium Falcon asteroid field transition (mechanical and perceptual shift)
Introduction of single-life characters (Han Solo, Princess Leia) on Death Star
Resource depletion scenarios (shields for asteroid field, Obi-Wan resurrections)

notable_level_patterns.description: Non-linear open-area exploration where player must locate and retrieve four character objectives (R2-D2, Obi-Wan, Han Solo, shields) in any order. Safe/moderate tension environment establishing core traversal and combat mechanics. Multiple lives for Luke allow risk tolerance.

Progression gate built on mandatory paradigm shift: character assembly on Tatooine unlocks first-person Millennium Falcon sequence. Requires resource collection (shields) from earlier play; gates access to final Death Star levels. Creates distinct tension escalation.

Death Star levels introduce playable characters with single-life pools (Han Solo, Princess Leia). Obi-Wan resurrection mechanic (5 uses total) becomes scarce resource. Each death is permanent unless resurrection available. Escalates stakes through attrition gameplay.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Death Star detention block rescue, Death Star tractor beam

notable_level_patterns.description: Final Death Star section chains multiple objectives (tractor beam destruction → Leia rescue → rebel fighter engagement) in sequence. No safe havens; sustained danger state until completion. Narrative pacing aligns with mechanical intensity.
